creator: fsbruva@yahoo.com
text:
This bug is actually with the contents of UPDATING.

The change in my patch has been overcome by event. On 18 Jan 18 with 10.2.12_1 (r459320), the four headers got moved out of -server, and into -client. The problem is that this created a dependencies situation that required manual intervention. 

You need to upgrade -server to remove the ownership of those four headers. You can't upgrade server without upgrading -client. You can't install the new version of the client because the upgrade version has ownership of the four headers. You need to upgrade -server to remove the ownership of those four headers.... and on... and on.

The only way past this pkg-plist change is (after you make a BACKUP!!!):
# pkg delete mariadb102-server
# portmaster databases/mariadb102-server

This will remove the ownership of the four headers via the deinstall of -server, and then the install of -server will upgrade -client.
